methodology_sections: 1.1 Asset Inventory - Financial assets, state assets, privilege assets, reputation assets
1.3 System Architecture Mapping - Contract hierarchies, dependencies, upgrade patterns, access control
2.1 Entry Point Enumeration - Public/external functions, callbacks, parameter analysis

financial_assets: ETH held/transferred by Tokenomics-related Treasury (external) and by DefaultTargetDispenserL2 variants via receive()/drain() (native balances on L2 dispensers)

OLAS ERC20 balances held by Dispenser (temporarily minted to self for bridging), L1 DepositProcessors (allowances/approvals), L2 TargetDispensers (received bridged OLAS, deposited into staking targets, or migrated)
ERC20 staking rewards held by StakingToken contracts (stakingToken balance tracked by `balance`/`availableRewards`)
Native-token staking rewards held by StakingNativeToken contracts (`address(this).balance` tracked by `balance`/`availableRewards`)
Service Registry ERC721 tokens staked into StakingBase implementations (service NFTs held by staking contracts during staking)
Cross-chain bridge message fees (msg.value) forwarded by Dispenser to DepositProcessors; msg.value used in L2 dispensers to send withheld sync messages back to L1
Withheld OLAS amounts per chain (Dispenser.mapChainIdWithheldAmounts, DefaultTargetDispenserL2.withheldAmount) representing deferred/retained emission value
state_assets: Tokenomics epoch state: epochCounter, epochLen, timeLaunch, currentYear, inflationPerSecond, maxBond, effectiveBond, veOLASThreshold/nextVeOLASThreshold, nextEpochLen, epsilonRate, devsPerCapital, codePerDev, lastDonationBlockNumber
Tokenomics per-epoch accounting: mapEpochTokenomics[epoch].epochPoint (donations, inflation, idf, endTime, fractions) and unitPoints (sumUnitTopUpsOLAS, numNewUnits, reward/topUp fractions)

Tokenomics incentive ledgers: mapUnitIncentives[unitType][unitId] (reward/topUp + pending relatives + lastEpoch), mapNewUnits, mapNewOwners, mapOwnerRewards/mapOwnerTopUps (declared but not used in shown code), mapServiceAmounts (declared but not used in shown code)
Tokenomics service staking accounting: mapEpochStakingPoints[epoch] (stakingIncentive, maxStakingIncentive, minStakingWeight, stakingFraction)
Dispenser configuration: owner, paused state, tokenomics/treasury/voteWeighting addresses, maxNumClaimingEpochs, maxNumStakingTargets, mapChainIdDepositProcessors, mapChainIdWithheldAmounts

VoteWeighting governance state: setNominees/setRemovedNominees, mapNomineeIds/mapRemovedNominees, pointsWeight/changesWeight/timeWeight, pointsSum/changesSum/timeSum, voteUserSlopes, voteUserPower, lastUserVote

StakingBase state per instance: initialization parameters (metadataHash, maxNumServices, rewardsPerSecond, minStakingDeposit, maxNumInactivityPeriods, livenessPeriod, timeForEmissions, numAgentInstances, threshold/configHash/proxyHash, serviceRegistry/activityChecker, agentIds[]), epochCounter, balance, availableRewards, emissionsAmount, tsCheckpoint, mapServiceInfo, setServiceIds
DefaultTargetDispenserL2 state: withheldAmount, stakingBatchNonce, stakingQueueingNonces, paused, owner, _locked
DefaultDepositProcessorL1 state: l2TargetDispenser (set once then owner=0), stakingBatchNonce, owner
StakingFactory state: nonce (CREATE2 salt), verifier, mapInstanceParams (implementation, deployer, isEnabled)
StakingVerifier state: limits, implementationsCheck, mapImplementations whitelist
privilege_assets: Tokenomics.owner: can change owner, managers (treasury/depository/dispenser), registries, blacklist, tokenomics parameters/fractions/staking params, and set tokenomics implementation slot (PROXY_TOKENOMICS)
Dispenser.owner: can change owner/managers, staking params, deposit processor mapping, sync withheld maintenance, pause state
VoteWeighting.owner: can change owner, set dispenser, removeNominee (powerful state change)
DefaultTargetDispenserL2.owner: can pause/unpause, processDataMaintenance, drain native, migrate (irreversible lock), changeOwner
DefaultDepositProcessorL1.owner (until setL2TargetDispenser): can set L2 target dispenser then becomes ownerless
StakingFactory.owner: can change owner, set verifier; Instance deployer privilege: can enable/disable instance
StakingVerifier.owner: can change limits, toggle/check implementation whitelist and statuses
Bridge relayer roles (implicit): L1/L2 message relayers and token relayers are trusted callers for receiveMessage/onTokenBridged/receiveWormholeMessages/receivePayloadAndTokens paths
Staking contracts: service owners control stake/unstake/claim flows for their serviceId; multisig address receives withdrawals
reputation_assets: veOLAS voting power allocations in VoteWeighting (pointsWeight/pointsSum) determining cross-chain emission distribution
Nominee registry (setNominees) representing supported staking programs/targets and their chain associations
Service staking positions (service NFTs staked) and their liveness/inactivity records affecting eviction (protocol reputation and fairness)
Protocol integrity of cross-chain withheld amount accounting (withheld sync correctness impacts trust in emission distribution)

system_architecture.inheritance_hierarchy: Tokenomics inherits TokenomicsConstants. StakingToken and StakingNativeToken inherit StakingBase (abstract) which inherits Solmate ERC721TokenReceiver. DefaultTargetDispenserL2 is abstract and is inherited by chain-specific L2 dispensers: WormholeTargetDispenserL2 (also TokenReceiver/TokenBase), GnosisTargetDispenserL2, OptimismTargetDispenserL2, PolygonTargetDispenserL2 (also FxBaseChildTunnel), ArbitrumTargetDispenserL2. DefaultDepositProcessorL1 is abstract and inherited by L1 deposit processors: ArbitrumDepositProcessorL1, WormholeDepositProcessorL1 (also TokenSender/TokenBase), PolygonDepositProcessorL1 (also FxBaseRootTunnel), OptimismDepositProcessorL1, GnosisDepositProcessorL1. StakingFactory deploys StakingProxy (minimal UUPS-style proxy) which delegates to staking implementations (e.g., StakingToken, StakingNativeToken or others).
external_dependencies: Arbitrum Nitro/Token bridge interfaces (Inbox/Outbox/Gateway semantics via IBridge)
External system contracts: Treasury, VotingEscrow (veOLAS), ServiceRegistry, Component/Agent registries, DonatorBlacklist, ActivityChecker, StakingFactory verifier
system_architecture.upgrade_patterns: No Diamond pattern. Tokenomics uses a non-standard proxy marker slot (PROXY_TOKENOMICS) to enforce `checkpoint()` must be delegatecalled via a proxy; it also allows owner to write an implementation address into that slot, but no proxy contract is shown here. Staking instances are deployed behind StakingProxy which stores implementation at a fixed slot (SERVICE_STAKING_PROXY) and delegates all calls (UUPS-like proxy without built-in upgrade function; upgrading would require an implementation-exposed upgrade method, not shown). DefaultDepositProcessorL1 becomes ownerless after setting l2TargetDispenser (one-time configuration). L2 TargetDispenser has a `migrate()` escape hatch to move funds to a new dispenser and permanently lock itself.
system_architecture.access_control_structure: Predominantly single-owner pattern via `owner` state variable checks (Tokenomics, Dispenser, VoteWeighting, StakingFactory, StakingVerifier, Default* processors/dispensers). Some manager-only checks are address-equality based (Tokenomics: treasury/depository/dispenser; Dispenser: voteWeighting and depositProcessor per chain; DepositProcessors: only l1Dispenser can call sendMessage*; L2 dispensers: only specific relayer/sender combos). StakingBase uses per-service ownership (ServiceInfo.owner) for claim/unstake; serviceRegistry enforces ERC721 ownership on stake/transfer.
integration_points_with_other_protocols: Tokenomics <-> ServiceRegistry / ComponentRegistry / AgentRegistry (exists, getUnitIdsOfService, ownerOf, totalSupply)
L2 TargetDispensers <-> StakingFactory verifier (verifyInstanceAndGetEmissionsAmount) and staking targets (deposit)

state_changes: mapEpochTokenomics[epochCounter+1] parameter copying if no updates
mapEpochStakingPoints[epochCounter+1] parameter copying if no updates
mapUnitIncentives[*][*].pendingRelativeReward (maybe finalized to reward then zeroed in finalize)
mapUnitIncentives[*][*].pendingRelativeTopUp (maybe finalized to topUp then zeroed in finalize)
mapUnitIncentives[*][*].lastEpoch (set to 0 when finalized in this path)
Tokenomics incentive state via external call
NONE directly (but makes external checkpoint call and may be used by non-static callers); writes occur in callers when mapLastClaimedStakingEpochs updated
OLAS balance of this contract (minted via treasury + transferred to depositProcessor)
OLAS balance of this contract (minted via treasury + transferred to many depositProcessors)

external_calls: _withdraw(multisig,reward) (token transfer or native send in derived)

state_changes: mapNomineeIds (cleared and reassigned for swapped nominee)
ERC20 allowance to target via approve
OLAS balance moved into staking target via deposit
ERC20 allowances/OLAS transfers to staking targets via approve+deposit
paused (must be 2 prior, but not changed here)

upgradeability_surface.notes: Not a standard proxy implementation, but uses custom storage slot PROXY_TOKENOMICS: checkpoint() requires that slot to be nonzero (delegatecall-only sentinel). Owner can write arbitrary address to slot via changeTokenomicsImplementation(). If deployed behind a proxy, storage layout must match proxy; if used standalone, checkpoint() will always revert unless slot is manually set.

Minimal delegatecall proxy storing implementation at fixed slot SERVICE_STAKING_PROXY. No upgrade function in proxy; upgrades (if any) must be provided by implementation. Fallback delegates all calls; no receive() defined.

Intended to be used behind StakingProxy via factory. initialize() is external and unrestricted; protection is only single-init check. If deployed standalone, anyone can initialize first and seize configuration.
Intended to be used behind StakingProxy via factory. initialize() is external and unrestricted; single-init guard only.
Factory deploys CREATE2 proxies; not itself upgradeable.

initializer_guards: owner revoked to address(0) after setL2TargetDispenser

upgradeability_surface.notes: Has migrate() to move funds and permanently lock contract (owner=0, _locked=2). Not a proxy pattern but an operational upgrade/migration surface.
